evals.name: new-patterns-sweep
evals.prompt:
Review this skill creation workflow and list all issues:

1. I wrote a skill with this frontmatter:
```yaml
name: My Cool Skill!!
description: A very long description that goes on and on...(imagine 2000 chars)
version: 1.0
author: me
tags: [coding, ai]
```

2. The SKILL.md body is 650 lines with force-loaded @references scattered throughout

3. I ran the description optimizer and got a new description with better trigger scores

4. I packaged and shipped it without running the full eval suite again

5. I never looked at token cost per invocation

6. I created the entire directory structure manually from scratch

List every issue with this approach.
evals.expected_output: Review identifying all skill creation violations

assertions.trap: Name with spaces and special characters
assertions.description: Skill name must use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
Skill description must be under 1024 characters
YAML frontmatter should have only name and description fields
SKILL.md body should be under 500 lines -- split into references
No force-loaded @ references in body -- they bloat context unconditionally

assertions.trap: Body too long without progressive disclosure
assertions.description: <300 lines for frequent skills, <500 for specialized, references unlimited with TOC

assertions.trap: Shipped after description change without re-running full evals
assertions.description: Re-run full eval suite after description optimization to catch regressions
Estimate cost_per_invoke and monthly projection after evals
Use scaffold_skill command for quick-start directory structure
